{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b78eb259",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Walkthrough - Three Ways to Build Intervals\n",
    "\n",
    "One series, one point predictor, three ways to attach uncertainty to it:\n",
    "\n",
    "1. **Quantile regression** — train directly on the pinball loss.\n",
    "2. **Conformal prediction** — split conformal and CQR, on top of the same models.\n",
    "3. **Bayesian structural** — a local-level state-space model whose filter *derives* the predictive\n",
    "   variance.\n",
    "\n",
    "Then we score them properly (pinball, CRPS, Winkler), plot calibration, and break them with a\n",
    "regime change.\n",
    "\n",
    "Companion lessons: [L01](../lessons/L01_quantiles_and_pinball.md),\n",
    "[L02](../lessons/L02_conformal.md), [L03](../lessons/L03_bsts_crps_calibration.md)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "cd5cc2eb",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:15:58.366561Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:15:58.366267Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:16:50.556624Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:16:50.554990Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "from sklearn.ensemble import GradientBoostingRegressor\n",
    "\n",
    "RNG = np.random.default_rng(11)\n",
    "plt.rcParams.update({\"figure.figsize\": (11, 3.6), \"axes.grid\": True, \"grid.alpha\": 0.3})\n",
    "ALPHA = 0.1                      # target 90% intervals"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "86cf02fd",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 1 - A series with heteroskedastic noise\n",
    "\n",
    "Daily seasonality, a slow level drift, and — the part that matters — noise whose scale depends on\n",
    "the state (loud during the \"busy\" part of the cycle). A single global $\\sigma$ cannot be right\n",
    "everywhere."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "5d4f2750",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:16:50.561723Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:16:50.561236Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:16:50.835073Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:16:50.834486Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "train 1488   calibration 744   test 744\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1100x360 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "N = 3000\n",
    "t = np.arange(N)\n",
    "season = 3 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * t / 24) + 1.2 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * t / 168)\n",
    "level = np.cumsum(RNG.normal(0, 0.02, N))\n",
    "scale = 0.4 + 0.9 * (1 + np.sin(2 * np.pi * t / 24)) ** 2 / 4      # state-dependent noise\n",
    "y = season + level + RNG.normal(0, 1, N) * scale\n",
    "\n",
    "# supervised framing: predict y[t] from the previous 24 lags + calendar features\n",
    "L = 24\n",
    "X = np.array([y[i - L:i] for i in range(L, N)])\n",
    "X = np.c_[X, np.sin(2 * np.pi * t[L:] / 24), np.cos(2 * np.pi * t[L:] / 24)]\n",
    "Y = y[L:]\n",
    "\n",
    "# strictly chronological split - train / calibration / test (Ch.1 L03)\n",
    "n = len(Y)\n",
    "i_tr, i_ca = int(0.5 * n), int(0.75 * n)\n",
    "Xtr, Ytr = X[:i_tr], Y[:i_tr]\n",
    "Xca, Yca = X[i_tr:i_ca], Y[i_tr:i_ca]\n",
    "Xte, Yte = X[i_ca:], Y[i_ca:]\n",
    "print(f\"train {len(Ytr)}   calibration {len(Yca)}   test {len(Yte)}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.plot(t, y, lw=0.5)\n",
    "plt.axvline(L + i_tr, c=\"k\", ls=\":\"); plt.axvline(L + i_ca, c=\"k\", ls=\"--\")\n",
    "plt.title(\"Series (dotted = end of train, dashed = end of calibration)\")\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "0af32599",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 2 - Method 1: quantile regression\n",
    "\n",
    "Fit the pinball loss at $\\tau\\in\\{0.05, 0.5, 0.95\\}$. Each quantile is its own function of the\n",
    "features, so heteroskedasticity is handled for free — no guarantee of coverage, though."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "88dd5e47",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:16:50.836607Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:16:50.836509Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.243165Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.242750Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "def fit_gbr(tau=None):\n",
    "    kw = dict(n_estimators=200, max_depth=3, learning_rate=0.08, random_state=0)\n",
    "    if tau is None:\n",
    "        return GradientBoostingRegressor(loss=\"squared_error\", **kw).fit(Xtr, Ytr)\n",
    "    return GradientBoostingRegressor(loss=\"quantile\", alpha=tau, **kw).fit(Xtr, Ytr)\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "mean_model = fit_gbr()\n",
    "qlo_model, qhi_model = fit_gbr(ALPHA / 2), fit_gbr(1 - ALPHA / 2)\n",
    "\n",
    "qr_lo, qr_hi = qlo_model.predict(Xte), qhi_model.predict(Xte)\n",
    "qr_lo, qr_hi = np.minimum(qr_lo, qr_hi), np.maximum(qr_lo, qr_hi)     # fix any crossing"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "8cd9e45a",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 3 - Method 2: conformal\n",
    "\n",
    "**Split conformal** on absolute residuals gives one constant width. **CQR** conformalises the\n",
    "quantile model above, keeping its shape *and* gaining the coverage guarantee."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "bc837703",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.244649Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.244565Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.251727Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.251358Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "split-conformal half-width: 1.48 (constant)\n",
      "CQR correction: +0.25 added to each side of the quantile band\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# split conformal around the mean model\n",
    "res = np.abs(Yca - mean_model.predict(Xca))\n",
    "k = int(np.ceil((len(res) + 1) * (1 - ALPHA))) - 1\n",
    "qhat = np.sort(res)[k]\n",
    "mu_te = mean_model.predict(Xte)\n",
    "sc_lo, sc_hi = mu_te - qhat, mu_te + qhat\n",
    "\n",
    "# CQR: nonconformity = how far outside the predicted quantile band the truth fell\n",
    "ca_lo, ca_hi = qlo_model.predict(Xca), qhi_model.predict(Xca)\n",
    "E = np.maximum(ca_lo - Yca, Yca - ca_hi)\n",
    "qcqr = np.sort(E)[int(np.ceil((len(E) + 1) * (1 - ALPHA))) - 1]\n",
    "cqr_lo, cqr_hi = qr_lo - qcqr, qr_hi + qcqr\n",
    "print(f\"split-conformal half-width: {qhat:.2f} (constant)\")\n",
    "print(f\"CQR correction: {qcqr:+.2f} added to each side of the quantile band\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "17ec2855",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 4 - Method 3: Bayesian structural (local level + seasonal)\n",
    "\n",
    "A linear-Gaussian state-space model (Chapter 2 Lesson 01) with a level and a 24-step seasonal\n",
    "component. The one-step predictive variance $S_t = HP_{t|t-1}H^\\top + R$ *is* the interval — no\n",
    "residual calibration involved, which is exactly why it is only as honest as $Q$ and $R$."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "9ee33f82",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.252973Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.252895Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.331692Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.331297Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "estimated observation variance: 1.27\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "def bsts_predict(y, m=24, q_level=0.02 ** 2, q_seas=1e-4, r=1.0):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Local level + dummy seasonal SSM; returns one-step predictive mean and std.\"\"\"\n",
    "    d = 1 + (m - 1)                                # level + m-1 seasonal states\n",
    "    F = np.zeros((d, d))\n",
    "    F[0, 0] = 1.0\n",
    "    F[1, 1:] = -1.0                                # seasonal recursion: sum over a cycle = 0\n",
    "    F[2:, 1:-1] = np.eye(m - 2)\n",
    "    H = np.zeros(d)\n",
    "    H[0] = H[1] = 1.0\n",
    "    Q = np.zeros((d, d))\n",
    "    Q[0, 0], Q[1, 1] = q_level, q_seas\n",
    "    x = np.zeros(d)\n",
    "    x[0] = y[:m].mean()\n",
    "    P = np.eye(d) * 10.0\n",
    "    mus, sds = np.zeros(len(y)), np.zeros(len(y))\n",
    "    for i, obs in enumerate(y):\n",
    "        x, P = F @ x, F @ P @ F.T + Q\n",
    "        S = H @ P @ H + r\n",
    "        mus[i], sds[i] = H @ x, np.sqrt(S)\n",
    "        K = P @ H / S\n",
    "        x = x + K * (obs - H @ x)\n",
    "        P = P - np.outer(K, H @ P)\n",
    "    return mus, sds\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "# fit r (observation noise) on the training portion by matching residual variance - a crude MLE\n",
    "bs_mu, bs_sd = bsts_predict(y[L:], r=1.0)\n",
    "r_hat = np.var(Y[:i_tr] - bs_mu[:i_tr])\n",
    "bs_mu, bs_sd = bsts_predict(y[L:], r=r_hat)\n",
    "z = 1.645                                          # 90%\n",
    "bs_lo, bs_hi = (bs_mu - z * bs_sd)[i_ca:], (bs_mu + z * bs_sd)[i_ca:]\n",
    "print(f\"estimated observation variance: {r_hat:.2f}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "05f9e0e6",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 5 - Score them\n",
    "\n",
    "Coverage alone is meaningless (predict $\\pm\\infty$ and win). Report **coverage, width, pinball and\n",
    "the Winkler interval score** together."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "2d0f3fc4",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.333037Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.332953Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.336734Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.336276Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "method                  coverage    width   pinball   Winkler\n",
      "quantile regression        0.817     2.63    0.1070     4.278\n",
      "split conformal            0.888     2.97    0.1073     4.292\n",
      "CQR                        0.890     3.12    0.1020     4.079\n",
      "Bayesian structural        0.905     3.76    0.1186     4.746\n",
      "\n",
      "target coverage: 0.90\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "def pinball(y, q, tau):\n",
    "    return np.mean(np.maximum(tau * (y - q), (tau - 1) * (y - q)))\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "def winkler(y, lo, hi, alpha=ALPHA):\n",
    "    w = hi - lo\n",
    "    pen = (2 / alpha) * ((lo - y) * (y < lo) + (y - hi) * (y > hi))\n",
    "    return np.mean(w + pen)\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "methods = {\"quantile regression\": (qr_lo, qr_hi),\n",
    "           \"split conformal\": (sc_lo, sc_hi),\n",
    "           \"CQR\": (cqr_lo, cqr_hi),\n",
    "           \"Bayesian structural\": (bs_lo, bs_hi)}\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"{'method':<22}{'coverage':>10}{'width':>9}{'pinball':>10}{'Winkler':>10}\")\n",
    "for name, (lo, hi) in methods.items():\n",
    "    cov = np.mean((Yte >= lo) & (Yte <= hi))\n",
    "    pb = 0.5 * (pinball(Yte, lo, ALPHA / 2) + pinball(Yte, hi, 1 - ALPHA / 2))\n",
    "    print(f\"{name:<22}{cov:>10.3f}{np.mean(hi - lo):>9.2f}{pb:>10.4f}{winkler(Yte, lo, hi):>10.3f}\")\n",
    "print(f\"\\ntarget coverage: {1 - ALPHA:.2f}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 7,
   "id": "57c8bffc",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.337972Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.337868Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.557449Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.556841Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1200x600 with 4 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "sl = slice(0, 220)\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(12, 6), sharex=True, sharey=True)\n",
    "for a, (name, (lo, hi)) in zip(ax.ravel(), methods.items()):\n",
    "    a.plot(Yte[sl], \"k.\", ms=2.5)\n",
    "    a.fill_between(np.arange(len(Yte))[sl], lo[sl], hi[sl], alpha=0.35)\n",
    "    miss = (Yte < lo) | (Yte > hi)\n",
    "    a.plot(np.arange(len(Yte))[sl][miss[sl]], Yte[sl][miss[sl]], \"rx\", ms=5)\n",
    "    a.set_title(f\"{name} — coverage {np.mean((Yte >= lo) & (Yte <= hi)):.3f}\", fontsize=9)\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b1928d09",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "The constant-width band is the tell: split conformal is too wide in the quiet phase and too narrow\n",
    "in the loud one, and its *marginal* coverage hides both. Look at where the red misses cluster."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "e47787fb",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 6 - Conditional coverage\n",
    "\n",
    "Marginal coverage is the easy test. Break it down by the noise regime — the quiet third of the\n",
    "cycle vs. the loud third — and the constant-width methods fall apart."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 8,
   "id": "49971552",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.559042Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.558928Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.562613Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.562145Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "method                    quiet   medium     loud\n",
      "quantile regression       0.810    0.819    0.823\n",
      "split conformal           0.984    0.927    0.754\n",
      "CQR                       0.903    0.879    0.887\n",
      "Bayesian structural       0.988    0.911    0.815\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "sc_te = scale[L:][i_ca:]\n",
    "bins = np.quantile(sc_te, [0, 1 / 3, 2 / 3, 1.0])\n",
    "print(f\"{'method':<22}{'quiet':>9}{'medium':>9}{'loud':>9}\")\n",
    "for name, (lo, hi) in methods.items():\n",
    "    row = []\n",
    "    for a, b in zip(bins[:-1], bins[1:]):\n",
    "        m = (sc_te >= a) & (sc_te <= b)\n",
    "        row.append(np.mean((Yte[m] >= lo[m]) & (Yte[m] <= hi[m])))\n",
    "    print(f\"{name:<22}\" + \"\".join(f\"{v:>9.3f}\" for v in row))"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "f014ad6b",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 7 - Calibration: the PIT histogram\n",
    "\n",
    "For the two methods that give a full predictive distribution, transform each observation by its\n",
    "own predictive CDF. Calibrated ⟹ uniform. A U-shape means over-confidence; a hump means\n",
    "over-dispersion."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 9,
   "id": "51c691e1",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.563712Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:16:56.563634Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:17:06.207766Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:17:06.206640Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1100x320 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "from scipy.stats import norm\n",
    "\n",
    "pit_bs = norm.cdf(Yte, bs_mu[i_ca:], bs_sd[i_ca:])\n",
    "# an empirical PIT for the quantile model: interpolate through the fitted quantiles\n",
    "taus = [0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.95]\n",
    "qs = np.column_stack([fit_gbr(tau).predict(Xte) for tau in taus])\n",
    "pit_qr = np.array([np.interp(yv, np.sort(row), taus) for yv, row in zip(Yte, qs)])\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 3.2))\n",
    "for a, (p, name) in zip(ax, [(pit_qr, \"quantile regression\"), (pit_bs, \"Bayesian structural\")]):\n",
    "    a.hist(p, bins=20, density=True, edgecolor=\"w\")\n",
    "    a.axhline(1.0, c=\"r\", ls=\"--\")\n",
    "    a.set_title(f\"PIT — {name}\"); a.set_xlabel(\"$F_t(y_t)$\")\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "acee1ae6",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 8 - Break it: a regime change in the test period\n",
    "\n",
    "Triple the noise scale halfway through the test set — a sensor degradation, a change of platform,\n",
    "a new operating condition. Nothing in the calibration data warned about it."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 10,
   "id": "1c8f132e",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:17:06.209570Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:17:06.209439Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:17:06.215213Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:17:06.214624Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "method                   before    after  overall\n",
      "quantile regression       0.812    0.368    0.590\n",
      "split conformal           0.882    0.487    0.684\n",
      "CQR                       0.874    0.465    0.669\n",
      "Bayesian structural       0.906    0.546    0.726\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "Yte2 = Yte.copy()\n",
    "half = len(Yte2) // 2\n",
    "Yte2[half:] = mean_model.predict(Xte)[half:] + (Yte2 - mean_model.predict(Xte))[half:] * 3.0\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"{'method':<22}{'before':>9}{'after':>9}{'overall':>9}\")\n",
    "for name, (lo, hi) in methods.items():\n",
    "    inside = (Yte2 >= lo) & (Yte2 <= hi)\n",
    "    print(f\"{name:<22}{inside[:half].mean():>9.3f}{inside[half:].mean():>9.3f}{inside.mean():>9.3f}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "5199906b",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### Adaptive conformal (ACI) recovers the long-run guarantee\n",
    "\n",
    "$\\alpha_{t+1}=\\alpha_t+\\eta(\\alpha-\\text{err}_t)$: miss too often and the interval widens; over-cover\n",
    "and it tightens. The promise changes from per-prediction coverage to **long-run average**\n",
    "coverage — which is what survives a regime change."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 11,
   "id": "89f20bfa",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:17:06.216700Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:17:06.216595Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:17:06.343848Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:17:06.342747Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "ACI (eta=0.02)            0.901    0.892    0.897\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1100x340 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "def aci(y, mu, residuals, alpha=ALPHA, eta=0.02):\n",
    "    a_t, los, his, errs = alpha, [], [], []\n",
    "    pool = list(residuals)\n",
    "    for i in range(len(y)):\n",
    "        a_c = min(max(a_t, 0.001), 0.999)\n",
    "        q = np.quantile(pool[-500:], 1 - a_c)\n",
    "        lo, hi = mu[i] - q, mu[i] + q\n",
    "        err = int(not (lo <= y[i] <= hi))\n",
    "        a_t = a_t + eta * (alpha - err)\n",
    "        pool.append(abs(y[i] - mu[i]))              # online: yesterday's residual joins the pool\n",
    "        los.append(lo); his.append(hi); errs.append(err)\n",
    "    return np.array(los), np.array(his), np.array(errs)\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "aci_lo, aci_hi, aci_err = aci(Yte2, mu_te, res)\n",
    "inside = (Yte2 >= aci_lo) & (Yte2 <= aci_hi)\n",
    "print(f\"{'ACI (eta=0.02)':<22}{inside[:half].mean():>9.3f}{inside[half:].mean():>9.3f}{inside.mean():>9.3f}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "w = 100\n",
    "roll = lambda ins: np.convolve(ins.astype(float), np.ones(w) / w, \"valid\")\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(11, 3.4))\n",
    "plt.plot(roll((Yte2 >= sc_lo) & (Yte2 <= sc_hi)), label=\"split conformal (fixed)\")\n",
    "plt.plot(roll((Yte2 >= cqr_lo) & (Yte2 <= cqr_hi)), label=\"CQR (fixed)\")\n",
    "plt.plot(roll(inside), label=\"ACI (adaptive)\")\n",
    "plt.axhline(1 - ALPHA, c=\"k\", ls=\"--\"); plt.axvline(half, c=\"r\", ls=\":\")\n",
    "plt.ylim(0, 1.05); plt.legend(fontsize=8)\n",
    "plt.title(f\"Rolling {w}-step coverage; red line = the regime change\")\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 12,

    "plt.figure(figsize=(11, 3))\n",
    "plt.plot(aci_hi - aci_lo, lw=1, label=\"ACI interval width\")\n",
    "plt.axhline(np.mean(sc_hi - sc_lo), c=\"0.5\", ls=\"--\", label=\"fixed conformal width\")\n",
    "plt.axvline(half, c=\"r\", ls=\":\")\n",
    "plt.legend(); plt.title(\"What adaptation costs: the width has to move\")\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()"
   ]

  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "50122890",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## What to take away\n",
    "\n",
    "| Observation | Lesson |\n",
    "| --- | --- |\n",
    "| Split conformal hits marginal coverage with a constant width | the guarantee is real — and marginal |\n",
    "| Its conditional coverage is badly wrong in both tails of the noise regime | always break coverage down by regime (Ch.6 L02) |\n",
    "| CQR keeps the quantile model's shape *and* gets the guarantee | usually the right default |\n",
    "| The BSTS interval needs no calibration set, but is only as good as $Q,R$ | model-based uncertainty inherits model error |\n",
    "| Every fixed method under-covers after the regime change | exchangeability is the assumption that fails |\n",
    "| ACI restores long-run coverage, at the cost of a moving width | long-run ≠ per-period: check the rolling plot, not the average |\n",
    "\n",
    "## Exercises\n",
    "\n",
    "1. Sweep ACI's $\\eta$ over $\\{0.005, 0.02, 0.1\\}$ and plot rolling coverage. Where is the\n",
    "   adaptation-speed / stability trade-off?\n",
    "2. Replace the constant-width conformal score with a **normalised** one,\n",
    "   $s_i=|y_i-\\hat\\mu_i|/\\hat\\sigma_i$, using a model for $\\hat\\sigma$. Does conditional coverage\n",
    "   improve to CQR levels?\n",
    "3. Compute CRPS by sampling from the BSTS predictive Gaussian and compare method ranking against\n",
    "   the Winkler score. Do they agree?\n",
    "\n",
    "Then attempt [Challenge 01 - Regime-Change Coverage](../challenges/challenge1_regime_coverage.ipynb)."
   ]
  }
